rankedItems.rationale: Specific numerical benchmarks: 4.4x-4.9x speedup on RTX 3090, 164-177.8 tps measurements, DFlash implementation details. Actionable for users with concrete hardware specs.
rankedItems.title: BeeLlama v0.2.0: 4.4-4.9x Speedup via DFlash on RTX 3090
rankedItems.summary: BeeLlama v0.2.0 delivers major performance improvements through optimized DFlash implementation. Qwen 3.6 27B achieves 164 tps (4.40x baseline), Gemma 4 31B reaches 177.8 tps (4.93x baseline) on single RTX 3090. Improvements from lower DFlash overhead, cleaner prefill handling, drafter K/V projection caching, and safer CUDA execution.
bullets: Qwen 3.6 27B: 164 tps (4.40x speedup) vs baseline
Gemma 4 31B: 177.8 tps (4.93x speedup) vs baseline
Prompt processing speed near baseline maintained
Stricter draft/target validation and reasoning boundaries
rankedItems.bottomLine: Production-ready speedups with maintained quality; enables real-time inference on consumer hardware.

rankedItems.rationale: Quantitative shootout with explicit metrics: 89.4% vs 94% token accuracy, 500x divergence calculation, speed tradeoffs measured. Demonstrates exponential error compounding in autoregressive models.
rankedItems.title: Qwen3-Coder Quantization Shootout: UD-Q5_K_M Wins on Quality
rankedItems.summary: Comprehensive quantization evaluation on Qwen3-Coder-Next across MXFP4_MOE, Q4_K_M, Q5_K_M, and UD-Q5_K_M formats using wikitext-2 (583 chunks). UD-Q5_K_M achieves 94% token accuracy vs 89.4% for MXFP4, translating to 500x better agreement by token 100. Only 10GB larger with negligible decode speed penalty.
bullets: UD-Q5_K_M: 94% token accuracy vs MXFP4 89.4%
5% accuracy difference = 500x divergence by token 100
Decode speed within 9% despite 22% larger size
Prefill/refill speed tradeoffs quantified per hardware
rankedItems.bottomLine: Token accuracy compounds exponentially; 5% precision difference becomes critical on long-context tasks.

rankedItems.rationale: Concrete release with specific model sizes (50M, 124M, 350M), training data (20B tokens), and reproducible code examples. First in SupraLabs scaling plan signals systematic approach to efficient models.
rankedItems.title: Supra-50M Released: Compact 50M-Parameter Causal Language Model
rankedItems.summary: SupraLabs released Supra-50M (BASE and INSTRUCT), a 50M-parameter model trained on 20 billion tokens of high-quality educational text using custom byte-level BPE tokenizer. Achieves competitive results on benchmarks despite being significantly smaller than comparable open models. Includes Supra-124M and Supra-350M variants with reasoning and coding capabilities.
bullets: 50M parameters trained on 20B tokens from fineweb-edu
Custom byte-level BPE tokenizer from 500k documents
BASE and INSTRUCT versions with code examples provided
Part of SupraLabs Scaling Up Plan roadmap
rankedItems.bottomLine: Systematic approach to efficient model scaling with reproducible training methodology and open-source release strategy.

rankedItems.rationale: Specific performance comparison: 30% faster TG than Unsloth IQ on 6GB VRAM laptop with detailed hardware specs and configuration. Concrete use case validation.
rankedItems.title: ByteShape Qwen3.6-35B-A3B: 30% Faster Than Unsloth IQ on 6GB VRAM
rankedItems.summary: ByteShape CPU-5 quant (Q4_K_S-4.22bpw, 18.3GB) achieves 30% faster token generation than Unsloth UD-IQ4_XS (17.7GB) on 6GB VRAM laptop with CPU offload. Tested on Asus ROG Zephyrus G14 (RTX 3060, Ryzen 7 5800HS) with 65536 context window and 2048 ubatch size.
bullets: 30% faster TG than Unsloth UD-IQ4_XS on same hardware
18.3GB model fits 6GB VRAM with CPU offload
65536 context window maintained
Slightly lower PP speed but better overall throughput
rankedItems.bottomLine: Practical speedup on constrained hardware; imatrix vs regular quants explain performance differences.

rankedItems.rationale: Novel algorithmic approach (Vector Policy Optimization) with concrete task improvements. Addresses inference-time search diversity—actionable for scaling systems.
rankedItems.title: Vector Policy Optimization: Training for Diversity Improves Test-Time Search
rankedItems.summary: VPO is an RL algorithm that trains LLMs to anticipate diverse downstream reward functions and produce diverse solutions, enabling better performance in inference-time search (pass@k, best@k). Treats rewards as vector-valued (e.g., per-test-case correctness). Acts as drop-in GRPO replacement. Across four tasks, VPO matches or beats scalar RL baselines, with gaps widening as search budget grows.
bullets: Vector-valued reward training vs scalar optimization
Drop-in GRPO advantage estimator replacement
Improves pass@k and best@k metrics
Unlocks problems GRPO models cannot solve
rankedItems.bottomLine: Algorithmic shift toward diversity-aware training; critical for inference-scaling search procedures.

rankedItems.rationale: Specific model size (14.1GB), context window (105k), perplexity metric (7.4040), and production testing over multiple days. Concrete quality measurements.
rankedItems.title: Qwen-27B-IQ4_KS: 14.1GB with 105k Context, 1.5-1.75x Faster
rankedItems.summary: Qwen3.6-27B-i1-IQ4_KS quantization (14.1GB) achieves 1.5-1.75x faster performance than previous IQ4_XS variant with comparable quality. Supports 105k context window with Q4_0 Hadamard KV cache. Perplexity: 7.4040 ±0.02773 over 12 chunks (65536 context). Tested in production workflows for multiple days.
bullets: 14.1GB model size vs 14.7GB previous variant
1.5-1.75x faster than UD-IQ4_XS
105k context window with Q4_0 KV cache
Perplexity 7.4040 on wikitext evaluation
rankedItems.bottomLine: Production-validated quantization with measurable speedup and quality parity.

rankedItems.rationale: Concrete metrics: F1 99%, 65MB ONNX int8 model, $5 total cost, runs in browser. Actionable security application with measured performance.
rankedItems.title: Prompt Injection Detector: F1 99%, 65MB, Runs in Browser
rankedItems.summary: Trained prompt injection classifier using ml-intern + DeepSeek v4 Flash. DistilBERT model achieves F1 99% on synthetic dataset, quantized to ONNX int8 (65MB), runs in browser with Transformers.js v3. Total cost under $5 using OpenRouter API. Trained on deepset/prompt-injections and Shomi28/prompt-injection-dataset.
bullets: F1 99% on prompt injection detection
65MB ONNX int8 quantization
Runs in browser with Transformers.js v3
Under $5 total training cost via OpenRouter
rankedItems.bottomLine: Practical security tool with browser deployment; demonstrates ml-intern agent effectiveness.

rankedItems.rationale: Technical deep-dive on asymmetric KV cache quantization with concrete precision loss measurement (1.3%) and memory savings (>50%). Addresses real performance bottleneck.
rankedItems.title: Asymmetric KV Cache (q8/q4): 1.3% Precision Loss, 50%+ Memory Savings
rankedItems.summary: Discussion of asymmetric KV quantization in llama.cpp (e.g., -ctk q8_0 -ctv q4_0). Standard asymmetric combinations cause prompt processing to offload to CPU, tanking performance. User evaluation confirms 1.3% precision loss while saving >50% memory vs f16/f16. Suggests including KV cache quant combos during compilation without FA_ALL_QUANTS.
bullets: Asymmetric q8/q4 KV cache saves >50% memory
Only 1.3% precision loss measured
Avoids CPU offload with proper compilation flags
Enables 105k context on 16GB VRAM
rankedItems.bottomLine: Practical memory optimization with minimal quality impact; compilation strategy matters.

rankedItems.rationale: Identifies real production gap (benchmarks vs robustness) but lacks concrete solutions or metrics. Signals important problem without actionable path forward.
rankedItems.title: Benchmark Performance Doesn't Predict Production Robustness
rankedItems.summary: Observation that benchmark performance often fails to predict production behavior. Systems score well internally but fail under ambiguous user intent, messy context, contradictory instructions, and long-running sessions. Current evaluation heavily rewards clean-task optimization over behavioral robustness. Seeks alternatives to standard eval pipelines.
bullets: Benchmarks miss ambiguous intent, messy context, contradictions
Long-running sessions expose robustness gaps
Standard evals optimize for clean tasks
Need for production-focused evaluation methods
rankedItems.bottomLine: Important gap identified but solutions remain open; signals need for robustness-aware evaluation.

rankedItems.rationale: Specific bug fix (VRAM leak in MTP models) with technical root cause (spec, ctx_dft, model_dft cleanup). Actionable for users experiencing crashes.
rankedItems.title: llama.cpp b9274: Fixes MTP VRAM Leak via Proper Resource Cleanup
rankedItems.summary: Build b9274 addresses VRAM leak in Multi-Token Prediction models. destroy() function in server_context_impl was not freeing speculative decoder (spec), draft context (ctx_dft), or draft model (model_dft). On each sleep/resume cycle, GPU resources accumulated without cleanup, eventually crashing with OOM. Fix ensures proper cleanup order to avoid use-after-free.
bullets: MTP models leaked VRAM on sleep/resume cycles
spec, ctx_dft, model_dft not freed in destroy()
Proper cleanup order prevents use-after-free
Resolves OOM crashes after minutes of use
rankedItems.bottomLine: Critical stability fix for MTP deployments; proper resource lifecycle management.
